summary: Good soundboard capture with solid first‑set finishes and a standout The Other One, but the brief, tentative Dark Star feels underwhelming.
review: The recording is a typical soundboard source that sounds clear overall, though periodic cable‑glitch noises give it a slightly rough edge. Musically the show offers strong moments—especially the first‑set closers (Ramble on Rose, Beat It On Down the Line, Deal) and a memorable Drums → The Other One sequence—but the second set’s Dark Star is unusually short and tentative, making the performance feel mixed.

ai_rating.rationale: Show quality is mixed (some highlights, notable weak spots) while recording quality is generally good but marred by occasional glitches; the weighted average yields a mid‑range rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear overall mix with balanced keyboards, but periodic cable‑glitch noises and a slightly tentative guitar tone on Dark Star.

standout_songs: Beat It On Down the Line
Victim > Dark Star > Drums > Beam > Healy
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set ends strongly with tight, energetic numbers; second set is uneven—highlighted by a compelling The Other One transition but hampered by an unusually brief Dark Star and less‑exciting Scarlet/Fire.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Guitar work on Dark Star feels tentative and very short; later he adds a nice Stella Blue lick toward the end.
band_member_comments.Keys: Vince's repetitive keyboard motif dominates the Dark Star segment; overall keyboard balance is better on this board tape than audience mixes.
band_member_comments.Drums: Drums segue into The Other One is praised as a highlight, though occasional cable glitches are audible.
